objects.name: Analytic 1728
objects.description: Correlates (1) acquisition of foreground or background location permission sufficient for continuous geolocation evaluation, (2) repeated location checks or registration of geofence monitoring in background or low-interaction states, and (3) transition into sensitive behavior only after the device enters, exits, or remains within a qualifying geographic region. The defender observes a causal chain where an application suppresses malicious or higher-risk behavior until a location-derived condition is satisfied, then initiates follow-on actions such as network communication, background processing, or protected resource access.

x_mitre_log_source_references.name: MobileEDR:telemetry
x_mitre_log_source_references.channel: application remains dormant, low-activity, or background-resident across non-qualifying locations and transitions into active execution only after geographic condition is met

x_mitre_log_source_references.name: android:MDMLog
x_mitre_log_source_references.channel: application granted ACCESS_FINE_LOCATION and, when required for background operation, ACCESS_BACKGROUND_LOCATION + capability state sufficient for persistent geolocation monitoring before later guarded activity

x_mitre_log_source_references.name: MobileEDR:telemetry
x_mitre_log_source_references.channel: application invokes geolocation or geofencing framework operations (e.g., location polling or geofence registration/evaluation) and sensitive framework activity begins only after region match or location threshold condition

x_mitre_mutable_elements.description: Correlation window between location evaluation, region transition, and guarded execution
Defines proximity, radius, or duration within region required before subsequent activity is considered geographically gated
Whether suspiciousness increases when background location permission is present and activity occurs outside foreground use
Amount of low-activity or dormant runtime before location-qualified activation
Baseline of legitimate apps expected to use geofencing or conditional location-based features
Whether execution should be considered higher fidelity only when it begins from background or without recent user interaction
Minimum outbound traffic volume used to distinguish meaningful post-match activity from benign telemetry
